game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: Tsuro is a digital board game where 2 to 8 players take turns placing path tiles onto a 6x6 grid board, using tap-and-drag controls to select and orient a tile before placing it adjacent to their current stone. After placing a tile, the player's stone automatically moves along the newly formed path until it reaches an endpoint, potentially connecting to other players' paths or leading off the board. The core objective in classic mode is to be the last player remaining on the board, as any stone forced off the edge is eliminated from the game. Difficulty scales with the number of players and the strategic complexity of AI opponents (with 3 distinct personalities), while progression is tracked through over 50 achievements for "Dragon Master" and new modes like "Loop Battle" and "Longest Path" offer alternative win conditions.
game_info.description: Navigate a strategic path-building game where you place tiles to extend your route across a 6x6 grid. Your stone automatically follows the paths created by each tile placement. Survive longer than your opponents by avoiding being forced off the board's edge. Plan ahead, block rivals, and create safe passages to become the last player standing.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move tile selection cursor on hand display
Space: Rotate selected tile 90 degrees clockwise
Z: Place selected tile on the board (only valid adjacent positions are highlighted)
Enter: Start game
Escape: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Basic tile placement and movement validation test. Verifies that tiles can be selected, rotated, and placed in valid positions. Tests that the player stone moves correctly along paths after tile placement. Confirms basic game mechanics work without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Randomly select tiles from hand and place them in valid adjacent positions. Rotate tiles occasionally to test rotation mechanics. Continue placing tiles until game reaches a natural conclusion (win or lose condition). Focus on exercising core mechanics rather than optimal play.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Game should run without crashes or errors. Tiles should be placeable in valid positions. Player stone should move along paths correctly. Game should eventually reach a GAME_OVER state (either win or lose). All core mechanics should function as designed.

TEST_2.test_description: Optimal winning strategy test against AI opponents. Tests the ability to survive and eliminate all opponents through strategic tile placement. Validates win condition detection and multi-player game logic. Ensures AI opponents behave intelligently and create meaningful challenge.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Analyze board state and opponent positions to place tiles that maximize survival probability. Prioritize tiles that create long, safe paths away from board edges. Position tiles to force opponents toward board edges while keeping own path safe. Use tile rotation strategically to create optimal path connections. Adapt strategy based on remaining opponents and board congestion.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player should successfully eliminate all AI opponents and achieve GAME_OVER_WIN state. Win rate should be high (>70%) demonstrating effective strategy. Player stone should navigate complex board situations without going off edge. Game should properly detect and handle player victory condition.
